answer: Task shape: single board node with four already-specified, dependency-free migration leaves, one shared output schema, and a deterministic join—classic intra-workflow lifecycle coupling (T1/T∞≈4, float fan-out). Executor: keep executor=workflow on that parent node (planning semantics: shared schema/quality envelope/single join); do NOT spawn a workflow hub or pipeline() on Codex-without-hub—this host only supports origin background Shell plus codex cli-worker dispatch; no workflow runtime primitive, so the orchestrator manually implements micro-scale ready-dispatch-join TFU while board retains workflow executor. Pre-dispatch route: reconcile board; use isolated writer worktrees; bind exact target, T1 floor, live qualification and same-floor fallback only. Per-leaf dispatch: agent create, real spawn, bind real pid/session handle, link task, only then task start—no handle/link means no in_flight. After every runtime becomes terminal: terminalize each agent, harvest artifacts, deterministically join into the shared schema, and independently verify diff/tests/acceptance/content hash; runtime terminal and worker self-report are not task done. Parent workflow becomes done/verified only after join acceptance passes; otherwise replan.
